es.meaning: On my own; by myself; for my part.
es.exampleTranslation: I prefer working on my own.

it.meaning: On my own; by myself; for my part.
it.exampleTranslation: I prefer working on my own.

bridge.note: Same ledger metaphor (your own account), possessive on opposite sides of the noun. A tidy little word-order mirror — and in both languages it also means 'in my opinion', quietly.

interference.es_to_it: Word order flips: Italian postposes the possessive (conto MIO); importing Spanish order gives 'per mio conto' — which Italians actually also say, but the fixed everyday form postposes.
interference.it_to_es: Italian order pulls 'por cuenta mía' — grammatical, marked; the neutral form is por mi cuenta.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
